research.status_summary: Nautilus (nautil.us) is actively publishing as of June 2026 — website updated 2 days ago, newsletter running daily, and print editions continuing — with no reported layoffs, closures, or acquisitions; it remains a small for-profit science magazine under NautilusNext Inc.
research.hiring_policy: Job listings on Indeed show remote roles (Associate Editor, Newsletter Editor, Deputy Editor) with Nautilus explicitly advertising fully remote work; no public information found on visa sponsorship or EOR policy, and as a very small US media company, EOR/sponsorship is unlikely.
recent_news: Site active as of June 2, 2026 with fresh editorial content; newsletter page refreshed April 2026 confirming ongoing daily curation operations.
Print Edition 56 published January 20, 2026, managed by Liz Greene — indicating steady cadence of 6 annual print issues continues.
No funding rounds, acquisitions, or layoffs detected in Apr–Jun 2026; Facebook page shows DAILY DOT LLC listed as page manager, consistent with NautilusNext/Daily Dot ownership structure since 2019.
research.growth_signal: No new funding or headcount signals in 2026; Nautilus remains a small subscription-driven (10,000+ paying members) niche science brand with stable but modest trajectory, showing no meaningful growth or contraction indicators.
research.fit_for_lior: Very low fit — Nautilus is a tiny editorial/journalism operation with no product, data, or AI roles; its openings are editorial (Associate Editor, Newsletter Editor), leaving no clear entry point for Creative AI, Product Analyst, or Vibe Coder profiles.
